alternatives.name: GfS Türwächter (mechanical + IoT alarm guard) — spring-loaded handle guard with EasyWave radio
alternatives.tradeoffs: EasyWave protocol lock-in — not interoperable with campus PSIM natively; 30 m wireless range requires repeaters in large buildings; battery replacement maintenance cycle; surface-mount visible protrusion on door handle

alternatives.name: Electronic Exit Control Systems (dormakaba SafeRoute, GEZE SecuLogic) — mains-powered electronic exit control
alternatives.tradeoffs: Requires mains power at door — major construction in legacy buildings; high per-door installation cost; licensed contractor required; single point of failure if power-dependent alarm

alternatives.name: Basic mechanical door prop alarms (Detex EC-100, Seco-Larm) — contact alarm, no monitoring
alternatives.tradeoffs: Local alarm only — no centralized monitoring; no Clery Act-compliant documentation; no battery status visibility; no IoT; security officer must be on-site to hear alarm

alternatives.name: Campus access control with alarmed exit hardware (ASSA ABLOY Aperio, Allegion Von Duprin panic hardware) — credential-based control with alarmed release
alternatives.tradeoffs: Requires full IT provisioning and credential management; high system complexity; wired infrastructure; designed for access control, not standalone exit alarm monitoring; high integration cost for legacy buildings

alternatives.name: Campus police patrol + manual inspection rounds
alternatives.tradeoffs: 2-4 FTEs for full 24/7 campus coverage; no real-time detection; manual compliance logs — error-prone; patrol frequency gaps at night; does not meet continuous monitoring expectations of fire marshals or insurance underwriters

alternatives.name: Do nothing / accept non-compliance
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero cost. Clery Act violation risk; NFPA/state fire marshal fine exposure (>$10K/violation); insurance liability exposure; no monitoring; reputational risk at accreditation review
